full_text:
ITEM 1

Fake Tech Inc. provides cloud analytics software to enterprise customers worldwide. Revenue grew 18% year over year driven by expansion in existing accounts and new logo acquisition in North America and EMEA.

Our platform integrates data warehousing, observability, and machine learning pipelines. We compete with large cloud vendors and specialized analytics providers.

ITEM 1A

Investing in our securities involves risks. If any of the following risks occur, our business could be harmed.

We depend on third-party cloud infrastructure providers. An outage at a key provider could interrupt service delivery and reduce customer confidence.

Cybersecurity incidents could expose sensitive customer data and trigger regulatory penalties. We invest in security controls but cannot guarantee prevention of all attacks.

Competition from larger vendors with broader product suites may pressure pricing and slow new customer acquisition.

Macroeconomic uncertainty may cause customers to delay purchases or reduce contract sizes.

Foreign exchange fluctuations affect reported results because a portion of revenue is denominated outside the United States.

Talent retention in a competitive labor market may increase compensation costs and slow product delivery.

Intellectual property disputes could result in costly litigation or licensing requirements.

Changes in tax law may adversely affect our effective tax rate and cash flows.

Supply chain disruptions for hardware appliances may delay customer deployments.

Open source license compliance failures could force product changes or damages.

Acquisitions may fail to integrate and may dilute shareholder value.

Product defects or downtime could damage brand reputation and increase churn.

Government contracting requirements may impose additional compliance costs.

Data residency regulations may limit cross-border service delivery.

Rapid technology shifts may obsolete portions of our platform investment.

ITEM 7

Total revenue for fiscal 2024 was $4.2 billion, an increase of 18% compared to fiscal 2023. Subscription revenue represented 92% of total revenue.

Operating margin improved due to sales efficiency and infrastructure optimization. We expect continued investment in research and development.

Gross margin expanded 120 basis points due to economies of scale in hosting costs.

Sales and marketing expense increased as we expanded enterprise coverage in APAC.

Research and development spending rose to accelerate AI-assisted analytics features.

General and administrative costs remained stable as a percentage of revenue.

Free cash flow conversion improved on higher collections and deferred revenue growth.

We repurchased shares under the authorized program while maintaining liquidity buffers.

Debt outstanding remains modest with no near-term covenant concerns.

Seasonality in bookings is strongest in the fourth fiscal quarter.

ITEM 8

The following table summarizes revenue by segment for fiscal 2024.

| Segment | Revenue ($M) | YoY Growth |
| Cloud Platform | 3100 | 20% |
| Support Services | 1100 | 12% |

Consolidated financial statements follow in the exhibits.
